title: Hosted and Self-Hosted Runners, Isolation, Capacity, and Compatibility · Stakeholder Delivery Mission
summary: An open-source public-service team. The team needs a fork-safe workflow, exact event and revision evidence, least authority, isolated runners, bounded capacity, failure diagnosis, and an accessible execution map. Planned artifact: Deliver Fork-Safe CI Trust and Runner Map with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace requirement, source revision, review, checks, build inputs, immutable artifact, deployment, observation, and recovery evidence as one delivery chain.
Keep pull_request_target work on trusted base code or inspect untrusted content strictly as data without executing its scripts, dependencies, actions, or artifacts.
Threat-model persistence, workspace residue, credentials, network reachability, daemon sockets, labels, groups, and repository eligibility for self-hosted runners.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain
steps.instruction:

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Runner Capacity Queue Cost
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-runner-capacity-queue-cost-20f6yo". Tailor its runner-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Runner Capacity Queue Cost.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Runner Capacity Queue Cost into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Runner Capacity Queue Cost, keep Ci Runner Capacity Queue Cost connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-runner-capacity-queue-cost-20f6yo
  - competency: ci-runner-capacity-queue-cost
    artifact: runner-policy.yaml
    decision: runner capacity queue cost
    environment: multi-platform Buildx release to a non-production registry
    fault-injection: make the service container start before it is ready
    retained-proof: OIDC claims provider condition service account IAM and audit entry
    case-key: 20f6yo
    change: |
      runner-image: ubuntu-24.04-reviewed
      action-runtime: node24
      minimum-runner: 2.327.1-or-later
      untrusted-work: github-hosted-ephemeral
      self-hosted: trusted-release-only
      network: segmented
      cleanup: single-job-destroy
      capacity: queue-budget-and-max-parallel
    verify: inspect runner version image release labels groups network authority queue duration and teardown evidence
    failure: Reject latest as immutable, reused public-PR runners, workspace cleanup as host destruction, or unbounded parallelism.
    repair: Record compatibility, isolate untrusted work, destroy ephemeral capacity, segment authority, and measure queue and cost.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
